summary:
Propagated nerve action potentials maintain their integrity due to effective node-to-node propagation via myelinated segments but are still susceptible to environmental conditions such as oxygen levels which affect cell function crucially; they aren't inherently unaffected by changes in membrane characteristics though these effects may vary based on specific neuronal types. The correct choice should acknowledge influence from external/internal variables impacting impulse consistency beyond just intrinsic electrical properties alone. 

The final boxed selection remains \boxed{D}, acknowledging its limitations within stated options while emphasizing broader understanding aligns better with actual physiological mechanisms involved.
